metadata.title:
RAILS ARE TOO
LIGHT FOR


ENGINES

pages.raw_text:
There Is to be general over
hauling of the rails on the line of
the Great Northern railroad on ac-
count of the numerous accidents
happening of late. The recent two
serious accidents which happened
near the Camden tunnel have hast
ened the company to remedy the
trouble by putting in complete new
and heavy rails all along the sys-
tenn where needed.


This Is particularly true Of rail
road yards where there are many
light rails in use. Railroad men
claim that the entire Great North
ern track is in poor condition and
that the cause Of the accidents is
that the engines are much too
heavy for the steel in the tracks.
The heavy engines cause the rails
to spread.


To rectlfy this the Great North
ern is going to use lighter engines
on passenger trains and break up
the long passenger trains into sec
tions. In a short time there will
be eight passenger trains on the
run instead of four as now. New
rails have been laid throughout the
line in Montana and as far west
as Chinook.
